⭐ Task  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 20 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-53  💭 0 comments  🧵 2 linked                          
                                                                                                                      
  # A/B test paywall variants via PostHog feature flags                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  analytics, backlog  👀 You are watching                
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Using PostHog feature flags, set up a 2–3 variant A/B test over the paywall screen. Each variant differs in headline
  copy and/or displayed price. Variants are assigned client-side via a PostHog flag evaluated on paywall load. Track  
  paywall_viewed , checkout_started , and subscription_completed events with a variant property on each.              
                                                                                                                      
  Depends on: Stripe paywall live (KAN-43), PostHog instrumentation live (KAN-51).                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Context                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  Provides data-driven optimisation of paywall conversion after the initial paywall and analytics are live. Even a    
  small lift in conversion rate compounds significantly with user acquisition scale.                                  
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • PostHog feature flag created with 2–3 named variants (e.g. control , price-anchor , social-proof )                
  • Paywall screen reads the active variant from PostHog and renders the corresponding copy and/or price display      
  • paywall_viewed event sent with {{{ variant }}} property on every paywall load                                     
  • PostHog experiment or funnel dashboard shows conversion rate per variant                                          
  • If the PostHog flag call fails or times out, the screen falls back to the default variant without error           
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  IS BLOCKED BY

  (FE + BE)  • Task • Medium • Idea

⭐ Story  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 20 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-52  💭 0 comments  🧵 0 linked                         
                                                                                                                      
  # 'Did you buy?' in-app follow-up survey                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  analytics, sprint-1  👀 You are watching               
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Show a one-time in-app survey banner after a user clicks a product buy link and later returns focus to the app.     
                                                                                                                      
  **Trigger** : listen for document.visibilitychange (state → 'visible' ); if the user has at least one un-surveyed   
  buy-                                                                                                                
  link click (tracked in component state or localStorage keyed by item ID), display the banner for that item.         
                                                                                                                      
  **Banner contents** : "Did you buy [item name]?" + Yes / No buttons + optional free-text reason field. Dismissing   
  without answering counts as a non-response and should not re-show.                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  **Frontend** : frontend/src/screens/Discover/Discover.jsx — within the ItemRow component or as an overlay managed by
  Discover.jsx .                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  **Backend** : new survey/ module — backend/src/survey/survey.controller.ts , survey.service.ts , survey.module.ts . 
  Endpoint: POST /survey/purchase-intent with body {{{ item_id: string, bought: boolean, reason?: string }}}.         
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Banner appears once per buy-link click when the user returns to the tab; does not re-appear for the same item on  
  subsequent returns                                                                                                  
  • Dismissing the banner (without answering) suppresses re-showing for that item                                     
  • POST /survey/purchase-intent persists the response and returns 201                                                
  • npm run test passes for the new survey service                                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Needs human input                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Should purchase_survey_responses be linked to the authenticated User (richer analysis, requires user to be      
  logged in on return) or stored anonymously with a session token? Please confirm the approach before building the    
  schema migration.                                                                                                   
  [ ] Confirm or adjust the suggested Prisma model fields: id (uuid) , user_id (uuid, nullable) , item_id (string) ,  
  bought (boolean) , reason (string, nullable) , created_at (datetime) .

⭐ Task  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 20 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-51  💭 0 comments  🧵 1 linked                          
                                                                                                                      
  # Analytics: PostHog instrumentation (FE + BE)                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  analytics, sprint-1  👀 You are watching               
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Add PostHog analytics instrumentation to the frontend and backend.                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  **Frontend** (initialise PostHog on mount in frontend/src/App.jsx ; fire events in the relevant screen components): 
                                                                                                                      
  • signup_completed — after successful registration; include {{{ gender, nationality }}} from the signup form        
  • vibe_extracted — after extractVibe() resolves in frontend/src/screens/Discover/Discover.jsx                       
  • outfit_generated — after stylePieces() resolves in Discover.jsx                                                   
  • buy_link_clicked — when a product buy link is tapped in Discover.jsx                                              
                                                                                                                      
  **Backend** (NestJS interceptor or middleware in backend/src/ ):                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  • api_call — every request with {{{ endpoint, status_code, duration_ms }}}                                          
                                                                                                                      
  **PII rule** : identify users by sha256(user.id) — never send the raw UUID or email address to PostHog.             
                                                                                                                      
  Note: paywall funnel events ( checkout_started , subscription_activated ) will be added as part of KAN-43 once the  
  paywall ships.                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  **Docs** : update backend/README.md with the POSTHOG_API_KEY env var and a short event catalogue.                   
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • All four frontend events appear in the PostHog live event stream after a full test run through the app            
  • The backend interceptor emits api_call with correct endpoint , status_code , and duration_ms fields               
  • POSTHOG_API_KEY is added to backend/.env.example and frontend/.env.example                                        
  • No raw UUIDs or email addresses appear in PostHog event properties                                                
  • backend/README.md lists the event catalogue                                                                       
  • npm run test passes                                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  BLOCKS                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  KAN-53 A/B test paywall variants via PostHog feature flags • Task • Medium • Idea

  # Styling preferences: personal color, occasion, explicit color anchor                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  backend, frontend, sprint-1  👀 You are watching       
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Add three optional styling preference fields to POST /style-pieces .                                                
                                                                                                                      
  **Backend** ( backend/src/style-pieces/ ):                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  • style-pieces.dto.ts — add optional fields to StylePiecesDto : personal_color_season?: 'spring' | 'summer' |       
  'autumn' | 'winter' , occasion?: string , color_anchor?: string (hex or colour name).                               
  • style-pieces.service.ts — when any field is provided, include it in the Sonnet prompt (e.g. "The user's personal  
  colour season is summer. Bias the palette toward cool, muted tones.").                                              
                                                                                                                      
  **Frontend** : add UI controls for the three fields at the step in the Discover flow agreed in the open question    
  below.                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • All three fields are optional; requests without them behave identically to before                                 
  • When color_anchor is set, the logged Sonnet prompt contains the anchor and the returned outfits demonstrably      
  favour that colour                                                                                                  
  • npm run test passes                                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ## Needs human input                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] At which step in the Discover flow should these preferences be collected? Options: (a) a new step shown between 
  vibe extraction and the pipeline run, (b) inline on the same screen as the celebrity name / photo inputs, (c)       
  collapsible "Advanced options" above the "Find my pieces" action. Please pick one so the FE layout can be built.

  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  frontend, sprint-1  👀 You are watching                
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Add a regenerate control to the ResultCard component in frontend/src/screens/Discover/Discover.jsx :                
                                                                                                                      
  • A short free-text input (placeholder: "e.g. less formal, swap shoes for sneakers") and a "Regenerate" button per  
  outfit card.                                                                                                        
  • On submit, call POST /style-pieces/regenerate (KAN-48) with the current outfit object and the feedback text.      
  • Show an inline loading state on the card while the request is in flight.                                          
  • On success, replace the outfit card in-place with the returned outfit — no full-page reload.                      
  • On error, show an inline error message and keep the original outfit card visible.                                 
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Submitting feedback visibly replaces the card in-place without a page reload                                      
  • An inline loading indicator is shown during the request                                                           
  • A network error surfaces as an inline message without removing the original card                                  
  • npm run build (in frontend/ ) passes

  # BE: Stripe webhook + entitlement check on protected endpoints                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  backend, monetization, sprint-1  👀 You are watching   
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Add Stripe integration to the backend. New module: backend/src/stripe/ (controller, service, module). Requires env  
  vars from KAN-41 ( STRIPE_PRICE_ID , STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET ) — add both to backend/.env.example .                   
                                                                                                                      
  **Three deliverables:**                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  1. POST /stripe/checkout-session — creates and returns a Stripe Checkout Session URL for the subscription Price ID (
  STRIPE_PRICE_ID ).                                                                                                  
  2. POST /stripe/webhook — verifies the Stripe-Signature header using STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET , then: sets User.       
  is_subscribed = true on checkout.session.completed ; sets User.is_subscribed = false on customer.subscription.      
  deleted .                                                                                                           
  3. SubscriptionGuard — checks User.is_subscribed ; rejects with HTTP 402 on unsubscribed users; applied to the      
  protected endpoints (see open question).                                                                            
  **Schema change** : add is_subscribed Boolean @default(false) to the User model in backend/prisma/schema.prisma and 
  generate a migration.                                                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Webhook handler is unit-tested: valid signature + checkout.session.completed → is_subscribed flipped to true ;    
  invalid signature → 400 returned                                                                                    
  • SubscriptionGuard unit-tested: rejects a request from a user with is_subscribed = false ; allows a request with   
  is_subscribed = true

⭐ Epic  🚧 Idea  ⌛ Sat, 20 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-36  💭 0 comments  🧵 0 linked                          
                                                                                                                      
  # Stripe paywall (monthly subscription)                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  monetization, sprint-1  👀 You are watching            
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Introduce a monthly subscription paywall via Stripe Checkout. Test mode first. Decomposed into three child tasks:   
  Stripe account and pricing setup (KAN-41), backend webhook and entitlement guard (KAN-42), and frontend paywall     
  screen                                                                                                              
  with Checkout redirect (KAN-43).h2. Context                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  Without a monetization layer the app cannot sustain itself. This epic gates the core styling features behind a
